fault_hypothesis: Line 77 uses strict greater-than (`now > adjusted_expiry`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= adjusted_expiry`), so when `now` exactly equals `adjusted_expiry` the entry is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

constraints.intent: When now equals adjusted_expiry the condition should evaluate to True (expired), but the strict > misses this boundary case.
Verify that the boundary condition now == adjusted_expiry__1 is detectable right after adjusted_expiry is computed.
Confirm that the comparison at line 77 must cover the >= case for correctness at the expiry boundary.
After the else branch of the expiry check, now must be strictly less than adjusted_expiry; if now == adjusted_expiry we should never reach here.
